CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, data VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data) VALUES (1, 'Test Data 1');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data) VALUES (2, 'Test Data 2');
CREATE TABLE replication_test (rep_id INT PRIMARY KEY, test_id INT REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO replication_test (rep_id, test_id) SELECT 1, id FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
INSERT INTO replication_test (rep_id, test_id) VALUES (2, (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE data = 'Test Data 2'));
CREATE TABLE dummy_table (dummy_id INT PRIMARY KEY, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO dummy_table (dummy_id, description) VALUES (1, 'Dummy Data 1');
INSERT INTO dummy_table (dummy_id, description) VALUES (2, 'Dummy Data 2');
INSERT INTO replication_test(rep_id, test_id) SELECT 3, id FROM test_table WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM dummy_table WHERE dummy_id = 1);
UPDATE test_table SET data = 'Updated Data' WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table);
DELETE FROM replication_test WHERE test_id = (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE data = 'Test Data 1');
SELECT * FROM replication_test WHERE test_id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE id > (SELECT MIN(id) FROM test_table));
